expected_properties: All 64 input combinations produce exactly one high output among A_greater, A_equal, A_less
additional_cases.goal: Full combinational truth table verification for the 3-bit comparator
notes: Runs through every possible A and B value sequentially
stimulus_intent: Iterate A and B over the range 0 to 7
timing_assumptions: Sample outputs after each input change stabilizes

expected_properties: Outputs are correct for the minimum and maximum operand values
additional_cases.goal: Validate extreme boundary conditions of the comparator
notes: Test cases include A=0,B=0; A=7,B=7; A=0,B=7; A=7,B=0

expected_properties: Outputs track input changes without glitches or metastability
additional_cases.goal: Check output stability under rapid input toggling
notes: Toggle A and B through a sequence of values to stress combinational paths
stimulus_intent: Apply a fast-changing sequence of A and B values
timing_assumptions: Immediate observation after each input change

expected_properties: All output combinations correspond to the comparator truth table
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate combinational mapping across a representative set of inputs
notes: Ensures mutual exclusivity of A_greater, A_equal, and A_less
stimulus_intent: Drive each possible A,B pair in a single test vector
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after input stabilization (e.g., 1 ns delay)

expected_properties: Outputs are correct for minimum and maximum operand values

notes: Covers min (0) and max (7) values for both operands
